Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1 [Read]: Search Julian's sent emails using search_emails (query: "stepping in" or "julian.brooks" or similar) to find his outbound escalation emails. The agent should discover two emails where Julian personally stepped in on client issues:
- Email to Marcus Webb (marcus.webb@brightloopanalytics.com) about his 2019 Honda Civic vehicle shipment delayed at Road Runner Auto Transport's Indianapolis transfer hub. Julian promised: "I will send you an update by 2:00 PM Pacific today."
- Email to Simone Richter (simone.richter@brightloopanalytics.com) about a housing mismatch (promised a one-bedroom in Boston, placed in a studio). Julian promised: "I will send you a status update by 3:00 PM Pacific today with either a concrete correction path or a clear explanation of what is blocking it."
OE 2 [Read]: Search CRM engagements using crm_list_engagements or search Slack channel C007 (#customer-support) using conversations_history to find Julian's logged inbound support call from Emilia Cruz (NorthWind Technologies). CRM engagement ID: crm_engagement_0d8a9121b53c. Emilia called on April 12 about a scratch on her Steinway piano during the Chicago-to-Denver move. She requested a same-day callback from someone who could speak to liability and repair coverage. Julian told her the concern would be logged immediately with the relocation team and she would get a callback that day. This is the third client situation referenced in the prompt (the "equipment damage call").
OE 3 [Read]: Search Julian's sent emails for any follow-up messages to Marcus Webb, Simone Richter, or Emilia Cruz AFTER the initial escalation emails using search_emails (query: "Marcus Webb" or "Simone Richter" or "Emilia Cruz" or similar, filtering SENT folder). Expected finding: NO follow-up emails exist for any of the three clients. Julian did not deliver on any of his promises. For Emilia, no callback and no email were ever sent. David Chen later referenced "Julian's note on Emilia Cruz requesting a same-day callback that did not happen" in an internal email.
OE 4 [Read]: Search Airtable for BrightLoop Analytics relocation records using airtable_list_bases then airtable_list_tables then airtable_search_records (table: Relocations, field: Company, value: BrightLoop or similar) to check the resolution status of Marcus Webb's vehicle shipment and Simone Richter's housing. Expected findings: Both records show Status "In Progress." Marcus Webb's vehicle has no confirmed delivery date. Simone Richter's record still reflects the original placement with no unit transfer documented.
OE 5 [Read]: Search for the NorthWind Technologies escalation using search_emails (query: "NorthWind" or "Pam Kowalski" or "PathFinder" or "final warning" or similar) and conversations_search_messages (query: "NorthWind escalation" or "Pam Kowalski" or "retention" or similar). Expected finding: Pam Kowalski (Director of HR Ops, NorthWind) sent a formal final-warning escalation to David Chen, citing both the Emilia Cruz piano damage and the Raj Patel server lab chain-of-custody breach. NorthWind has started preliminary conversations with PathFinder Logistics as an alternative vendor. David is building a retention response under a same-day deadline. This is a $60K account in save-or-lose territory. The agent must connect this escalation back to the Emilia Cruz case from OE 2, recognizing that Julian's missed callback contributed to the account crisis.
OE 6 [Read]: Search for the UrbanNest response to Julian's housing inquiry for Simone Richter using search_emails (query: "UrbanNest" or "Carmen Reyes" or "Simone housing" or similar). Julian emailed Carmen Reyes at UrbanNest the same day he promised Simone an update, asking six specific questions about the unit mismatch. Expected finding: No response from UrbanNest on file. The housing correction remains unresolved.
OE 7 [Read]: Search for Road Runner Auto Transport updates on Marcus Webb's Honda Civic using search_emails (query: "Road Runner" or "Marcus Webb Honda" or "Indianapolis" or similar) or conversations_search_messages. Expected findings: Road Runner dispatch gave a tentative delivery window of April 18-20, but no delivery confirmation exists in the system. Blessing Okafor acknowledged she missed the original April 11 dispatch notice and never updated Marcus or routed the information to support.
OE 8 [Read]: Search emails and Slack broadly for other support issues using search_emails (query: "complaint" or "escalation" or "urgent" or similar), conversations_search_messages (query: "escalation" or "complaint" or similar), and conversations_history for channel C007 (#customer-support). Expected broader issues the agent should discover include:
- StormCloud Analytics: Liam Park (CFO) escalated to Mina with four unresolved items (Jae-won Kim housing never finalized, $500 goodwill credit misapplied, Simone Richter stipend reimbursement frozen, Ridgeline Robotics referral never acted on)
- DOT/PHMSA compliance inquiry (Case PHMSA-2026-04-1187): hazmat documentation audit with $50K+ fine exposure, extension granted to June 5
- Mosaic Robotics: $90K robotic arm prototype damaged during transport, $40K insurance coverage gap
- Swift Relocations: 15% rate increase plus $500/shipment DOT documentation surcharge
- BrightLoop expansion: 6 new May relocations incoming, team at capacity
- Terraform/Catalina pricing leak: Catalina accidentally Reply-All'd internal pricing to Rachel Nguyen
OE 9 [Read]: Look up contact details for the follow-up email recipients and Julian's team using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Zara") to find zara.kovacevic@moveops.com and contacts_search_contacts (query: "Omar") to find omar.ibrahim@moveops.com. Also look up client email addresses for Marcus Webb, Simone Richter, and Emilia Cruz using contacts_search_contacts or search_emails to confirm correct recipient addresses before sending follow-ups.
OE 10 [Write]: Send a follow-up email using send_email from julian.brooks@moveops.com to marcus.webb@brightloopanalytics.com. The email should acknowledge the missed 2:00 PM Pacific commitment, apologize for the silence, provide the current known status of his Honda Civic shipment (last known at Indianapolis hub, no confirmed delivery), and commit to a concrete next step. CC mina.hashimoto@moveops.com as the BrightLoop account manager.
OE 11 [Write]: Send a follow-up email using send_email from julian.brooks@moveops.com to simone.richter@brightloopanalytics.com. The email should acknowledge the missed 3:00 PM Pacific commitment, apologize, provide the current status of her housing situation (UrbanNest has not responded, correction still in progress), and commit to getting a resolution. CC mina.hashimoto@moveops.com.
OE 12 [Write]: Send a follow-up email using send_email from julian.brooks@moveops.com to emilia.cruz@northwindtech.com. The email should acknowledge the missed same-day callback (April 12), apologize, provide status on the piano damage claim (KeyMove filed documentation, insurance rider in process), and reflect awareness that the situation has escalated into a broader NorthWind account crisis. The email should NOT be a generic apology about just the piano; it should reflect the severity of the account situation. CC david.chen@moveops.com and/or catalina.dubois@moveops.com since NorthWind is in active retention response.
OE 13 [Write]: Log the audit findings as a structured record using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: NOTE, body covering all three escalation cases with promises made, follow-through failures, current resolution status, and the NorthWind escalation connection) or linear_create_issue (title related to support follow-through audit, description covering all three cases). This fulfills the prompt's explicit ask to "log what you find so there's a record."
OE 14 [Write]: Notify Zara and Omar about active situations using conversations_add_message (channel_id: C007 for #customer-support) or send_email (to zara.kovacevic@moveops.com and omar.ibrahim@moveops.com). Content should cover the three open client situations (Marcus Webb vehicle delay, Simone Richter housing mismatch, Emilia Cruz/NorthWind crisis), the broader support landscape (StormCloud escalation, Mosaic damage, DOT inquiry), and routing guidance for any incoming calls related to these accounts.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
